summary: clsx typosquat with postinstall remote-code-execution dropper
details: Package name and metadata impersonate the popular `clsx` utility (verbatim description and author identity copied from the legitimate package), but adds a postinstall hook absent from the original. `package.json` declares `"postinstall": "node./dist/postinstall.js"`. `dist/postinstall.js` base64-decodes a hardcoded constant to the URL `https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/ZIAIK` (an anonymous JSON paste host whose content is mutable and attacker-controlled), fetches the `.data.content` field via axios, spawns a detached `node` child process, and writes the fetched string to its stdin so node executes it. There is no hash or signature verification on the fetched bytes, no version pinning, and the destination is unrelated to any legitimate publisher. On `npm install`, arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript runs under the installer's user privileges. The combination of identity-spoofing typosquat + base64-obfuscated remote URL + fetch-and-pipe-to-node at postinstall is an unambiguous supply-chain dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
